title: Northern Trust Corporation (NTRS) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Northern Trust Corporation (NTRS) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: A 4.0% free cash flow growth rate is projected, reflecting the mature nature of the custody business and steady, single-digit historical growth in assets under management and custody.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: An 8.5% discount rate accounts for the inherent systemic risks in the financial sector, balanced by Northern Trust's strong capital position and stable, fee-driven revenue profile.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: A 2.0% terminal growth rate aligns with long-term expectations for global GDP and inflation, suitable for a mature, deeply established financial institution.

thesisTitle: The NTRS Thesis: Value in Focus
thesisParagraphs: Northern Trust is one of the largest banking institutions in the United States and one of the oldest, catering primarily to corporations, institutional investors, and ultra-high-net-worth individuals. The core of its intrinsic value lies in its incredibly sticky client base, as the frictional costs of moving billions of dollars in assets to a new custodian are massive.

While the company generates substantial free cash flow—recently hitting $4.76 billion—its current valuation appears slightly stretched. The market may be overly optimistic about near-term growth prospects, as the custody business is inherently slow-moving and highly sensitive to broader macroeconomic conditions and global market levels.

faq.question: Why did Gemini assign a 4% FCF growth rate?
faq.answer: Northern Trust operates in a highly mature, slow-growing industry. A 4% rate reflects realistic, sustainable growth tied to long-term market appreciation and modest new client acquisition.

faq.question: Is Northern Trust significantly overvalued?
faq.answer: The analysis suggests it is mildly overvalued by roughly 11%. It is not grossly mispriced, but the current price offers limited margin of safety.

faq.question: What is the biggest risk to Northern Trust's value?
faq.answer: A severe, prolonged global market downturn is the biggest risk, as it would mechanically reduce the value of assets under custody, directly impacting fee revenue.
